alerts.title: U.S. and Iran resume kinetic escalation; ceasefire collapses June 10-11
alerts.summary: After June 9-10 ceasefire, U.S. military launched new strikes on multiple Iranian targets June 10-11 (NPR, PBS, Axios, CNBC). Iran retaliated with attacks on Bahrain, Kuwait, and Jordan. Secretary Hegseth announced intention to strike Iran again. Multiple incidents clustered around Persian Gulf and central Iran (31.8°N, 52.8°E). Ceasefire has definitively broken.
alerts.region: Iran, Strait of Hormuz, Persian Gulf, Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan

alerts.title: Iran enrichment at 60% U-235; breakout window narrows to 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: IAEA confirmed Iran's uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with breakout time estimated at 1-2 weeks (Reuters, Jerusalem Post, IAEA board resolution, IranWire). IAEA board demanded Iran report uranium stockpile transparency and grant inspector access. Enrichment threshold now poses imminent proliferation risk concurrent with active kinetic escalation.

alerts.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17; Tyre and Sidon targeted
alerts.summary: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 17 civilians in Tyre and Sidon areas, with eight killed in single Tyre attack per BBC and Guardian reports (June 10-11). Strikes targeted populated civilian quarters, prompting evacuation orders. Represents escalation of Israel-Hezbollah kinetic phase beyond Iran-Israel axis.

alerts.title: Iran fires missiles at Israel during June 10-11 escalation cycle
alerts.summary: Iran launched direct missile attack on Israel on June 10-11, breaking April ceasefire framework (NPR, Axios, Washington Post, New York Times). Attack marked first such direct bombardment since fragile ceasefire took effect. Israel responded with counter-strikes on Iranian targets. Both sides signaled intent to continue if provoked.

alerts.title: U.S. sentiment on Iran conflict spikes negative to 58%; threat level peaks at CRITICAL (9/10)
alerts.summary: U.S. sentiment analysis shows 58% negative vs. 38% positive (4% neutral) with 'trump,' 'iran,' 'strikes,' and 'military' dominating trending terms (78, 31, 16, and 9 mentions respectively). Global threat level elevated to CRITICAL (9/10) per metrics dashboard. Brent crude oil at $94.50/bbl reflects escalation premium. Proxy forces (Hezbollah, Houthis, PMF) all elevated or active.

analysis_note: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire has definitively collapsed as of June 10-11 with renewed U.S. strikes, Iranian retaliation on Gulf states and Israel, and Israeli counter-escalation into Lebanon; nuclear breakout window has compressed to 1-2 weeks, creating a dual-domain crisis (kinetic + proliferation) at maximum strategic risk.
